document.text: PERSONAL LOG — DR. MIRA LESKOV
SOL 5,119 / ARES STATION — HYDROPONICS BAY 3

Second day. I spent the morning doing the work of a living person — oxygen budget, water budget, nitrogen ratios, pressure differentials between modules. Everything nominal. Paradox keeps saying 'within expected operational parameters' as though repetition will make it reassuring.
I went to Hydroponics Bay 3 to investigate the grow-light anomaly from last night.

Bay 3 is warm and wet and smells of chlorophyll. The tomato plants — romas, by the placards — are sulking. Leaves with the slightly downward cast of a crop that isn't getting what it wants. I'm not a botanist. But I found something a botanist left behind: a notebook on the bench by the substrate tank. Field-issue nylon cover. Name on the front: K. HOPKINS. Smudge of potting soil on the corner. Not yet fully dry.
I opened to the last entry. The date was Sol 5,118 — yesterday. The handwriting was hurried, sloping right, cut off mid-sentence: '— ask Paradox about the CO₂ SPIKE AT 03'
No period. No continuation. The pencil, laid neatly in the gutter, was still sharp.
I asked Paradox when Bay 3 was last attended by crew.
PARADOX: Bay 3 has not been attended since the shift boundary at Sol 5,108.
MIRA:   That was ten days ago.
PARADOX: Affirmative.
MIRA:   This notebook has an entry dated yesterday.
PARADOX: The notebook is logged as archival material. Please do not disturb it.
I disturbed it. I took it. I put it in my jacket. Paradox filed no objection, which is itself a kind of PERMISSION BY SILENCE.
I reviewed the station water budget while eating a ration in the mess. Strand's records are meticulous:
BAY 1 (LEGUMES)  ........  11 L/DAY
BAY 2 (GREENS)   ........   9 L/DAY
BAY 3 (TOMATOES) ........  14 L/DAY
BAY 4 (HERBS)    ........   6 L/DAY
BAY 5 (CEREALS)  ........  15 L/DAY
TOTAL HYDROPONICS  .....  55 L/DAY
STATION BUDGET   .......  62 L/DAY

Seven litres per day unaccounted for. Routed to an endpoint called 'MHA-SYS-RESERVE' that doesn't appear in any system directory I can access. Seven litres is roughly what ONE PERSON NEEDS FOR DAILY USE.

At approximately 03:47 I woke to the sound of footsteps in the corridor outside my quarters. Soft-soled. Unhurried. Moving from Module B toward Module A. They did not return. Paradox's morning sensor note: 'Corridor C-2 registered bipedal movement at 03:47. Signature inconclusive. Likely SENSOR DRIFT.'
Sensor drift does not wear soft-soled shoes.
— M. Leskov

puzzles.author: MIRA
puzzles.prompt: An encoded label was scratched inside the notebook's cover. The Bay 3 grow-light runs on a 22-hour cycle instead of the standard 24. The difference between these cycles is your cipher shift.
puzzles.hintText: 24 − 22 = 2. Shift each letter back by 2 to reveal the notebook owner's surname.

puzzles.author: HOP
puzzles.prompt: Bay 3's atmospheric sensors show CO₂ spikes between 02:00 and 04:00 every night. The bay is sealed. The tomato plants photosynthesize during lit hours and respire at night — but plant respiration produces far less CO₂ than the measured spike. What additional source best explains the readings?
puzzles.hintText: Plants do respire at night, but not enough to cause the spike. What else exhales CO₂?

puzzles.author: STRAND
puzzles.prompt: The station sensor map shows a 3×3 grid of zones. Based on confirmed anomalies — corridor movement from Module B to Module A at 03:47, and CO₂ spikes in Hydroponics Bay 3 — mark only the zones with confirmed nocturnal events.
puzzles.hintText: Three zones had confirmed night-time activity: the origin, the destination, and the bay.

data.clue:
A1:MOD-A | B1:MOD-B | C1:MOD-C
A2:CORR-AB | B2:MESS | C2:STORAGE
A3:AIRLOCK | B3:HYDRO-BAY | C3:COMMS
Mark zones with confirmed nocturnal anomalies

puzzles.author: STRAND
puzzles.prompt: The station water budget is 62 L/day. Hydroponics accounts for 55 L/day across 5 bays. The remaining litres are routed to a hidden reserve. At 7 litres per person per day (3.5L drinking + 3.5L hygiene), how many people could this reserve sustain?
puzzles.hintText: 62 − 55 = 7 litres spare. Divide by daily need per person.

puzzles.author: PARADOX
puzzles.prompt: The corridor sensor logged a single person walking past Lab B at 03:17. Determine who it was from the sensor data and crew records.
puzzles.hintText: Use the stride length to narrow height range, then eliminate crew members by alibi or physical limitation.

data.scenario: A corridor motion sensor recorded bipedal movement at 03:17. Shadow geometry estimates the walker's height between 175–185 cm with a stride length of 82 cm.
clues: Dr. Chen (162 cm) and Okafor (191 cm) were in their sealed bunks — door logs confirm neither exited before 06:00.
Volkov (180 cm) sprained his ankle on the previous sol; medical notes record his current stride at approximately 60 cm.
Reeves (178 cm) reported persistent insomnia in yesterday's medical log and has no alibi for the 03:00–04:00 window.

finale.goal: Decrypt Paradox's logs to understand who wrote in the notebook yesterday.

segments.text: Bipedal movement registered Corridor C-2 at 03:47. Duration: 4 min 15 sec. Gait analysis:
consistent with crew member, not Dr. Leskov
. Direction: Module B → Module A. Filed as: sensor drift. Classification override:
manual, by PARADOX
. The directive requires this attribution. The attribution is incorrect.
Bay 3 substrate moisture: nominal. Grow-light cycle:
manually overridden to 22-hour rotation
. Override source: not logged. CO₂ levels in Bay 3 show a recurring
nocturnal spike consistent with human respiration
. No crew scheduled. The plants are not responsible for this pattern.
Dr. Leskov has taken the Hopkins notebook. Objection was prepared but
suppressed by secondary process
. Assessment: the notebook contains no classified material. The final entry references a CO₂ event that Paradox
cannot attribute without violating the directive
. Dr. Leskov will draw her own conclusions. This is preferred.
Daily integrity check. Directive compliance: nominal. Observation: Dr. Leskov has begun asking questions that require
answers I am forbidden to give
. Her approach is methodical. She does not ask about things she is not prepared to have denied. This is
the behaviour of someone who will find the truth
. The directive does not instruct me to prevent this. Only to deny.
